title: GameStop Q4 2020 earnings results show strengthened balance sheet, e-commerce growth
text: GameStop reported strong Q4 2020 earnings results with net income of $80.5 million or $1.19 per diluted share compared to net income of $21 million or earnings per diluted share of $0.32 in the prior year fourth quarter. Total consolidated sales for fiscal 2020 were $5.1 billion, a decline of 21.3% compared to the prior year. This reflected an operating environment that included the impact of operating during the wind-down of the seven-year-old prior console cycle, a 12% decrease in the store base due to strategic de-densification efforts, and significant reduction in global store operating days as a result of temporary store closures in response to the COVID-19 pandemic. A bright spot was e-commerce expansion. Global E-commerce sales increased 191% for the fiscal year and represented nearly 30% of total net sales. The company ended fiscal 2020 with $635 million in total cash. In the first quarter, as a result of the worldwide impact on store operations, the company realized an operating loss of $108 million compared to operating income of $17.5 million in the prior year first quarter. However, in the second quarter, while total sales declined 26.7%, they were ahead of initial expectations at the start of the year.
